revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23993722414|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:جيسون ان]][[en:Jison-in]][[fr:Jison-in]][[it:Jison-in]][[ja:慈尊院]][[tr:Jison-in]] {{Infobox Buddhist temple|image=File:Jison-in Two-storey pagoda.jpg|religious_affiliation={{ill|Kōyasan Shingon-shū|qid=Q3077357|1=Kōyasan Shingon-shū}}|prefecture={{ill|Kudoyama|qid=Q1354976|1=Kudoyama}}|coordinates={{Coord|34.29519167|N|135.55018611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|founded_by={{ill|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|1=Kūkai}}|deity={{ill|Maitreya|qid=Q193461|1=Maitreya}}|part_of={{ill|Q9388421|qid=Q9388421|1=Q9388421}}; {{ill|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|qid=Q611345|1=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range}}; {{ill|Shinbutsu Reijō Junpai no Michi|qid=Q10444949|1=Sacred Sites of Kamigata}}}}
{{No footnotes|date=January 2024}} [[File:Jisonin hondo.jpg|thumb|Jison-in's ''Miroku-dō'']] [[File:Jison-in Two-storey pagoda.jpg|thumb|Jison-in's ''[[:en:Tahō-tō|Tahō-tō]]'', It was rebuilt in 1624]] {{nihongo|'''Jison-in'''|慈尊院}} is a {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|qid=Q3141900|lt=Buddhist temple|1=Buddhist temples in Japan}} in the town of [[:en:Kudoyama, Wakayama|Kudoyama]] that marks the entrance to the pilgrimage route of [[:en:Koyasan|Koyasan]].
==Temple== It is part of the {{ill|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|en|Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|qid=Q611345|lt=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range|1=Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range}} [[:en:UNESCO World Heritage Site|UNESCO World Heritage Site]].
The Koyasan complex includes: * [[:en:Kongobu-ji|Kongobu-ji]], built by {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|qid=Q222227|lt=Kūkai|1=Kūkai}} in 816 as the principal stage for esoteric Buddhism on an 800m high mountain basin, * Jison-in, built as an administrative office to facilitate the construction and management of Kongobu-ji, * {{ill|Niukanshōfu Shrine|en|Niukanshōfu Shrine|qid=Q3342246|lt=Niukanshofu Jinja|1=Niukanshōfu Shrine}}, constructed as a guardian shrine to protect the Niukanshofu estate of Kongobu-ji, and * [[Niutsuhime Shrine|Niutsuhime Jinja]], situated in the Amano basin between Kongobu-ji and Jison-in. Closely connected to Koyasan, it enshrines Koya Myōjin who, legend tells, gave land to Kūkai when he choose the compound of Kongobu-ji, and Niu Myōjin, who guided him, and all of them are connected by the pilgrimage route Koyasan Choishimichi.
==See also== *{{ill|List of National Treasures of Japan (sculptures)|en|List of National Treasures of Japan (sculptures)|qid=Q3245521|lt=List of National Treasures of Japan (sculptures)|1=List of National Treasures of Japan (sculptures)}}
==Sources== {{Commons Category|Jisonin}} *[https://web.archive.org/web/20070706025658/http://www.sekaiisan-wakayama.jp/english/s_kouya.htm Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range ]
{{Coord|34|17|42.69|N|135|33|0.67|E|region:JP_source:jawiki|display=title}} {{Buddhist temples in Japan}} {{Authority control}}
[[en:Jison-in]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[arz:جيسون ان]] {{Commons category|Jisonin}} [[fr:Jison-in]] [[it:Jison-in]] [[ja:慈尊院]] {{wikidata link|Q3179279}} [[Category:Buddhist temples in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kōyasan]] [[Category:Kōyasan Shingon temples]] [[Category:Wakayama Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:World Heritage Sites in Japan]]
